name: Count Length, Then Remove
summary: First walk the list to count its length, then walk (length - n) steps from a dummy head and unlink that node.
visualizationIntent: init: build the list with a dummy sentinel (special) before the head. pass 1 (count): curr walks the whole list counting length. pass 2 (locate): curr resets to the dummy and walks (length - n) steps to sit just before the target node. update: the node after curr is the one to remove — it flashes, curr's next is rewired to skip it (a changed link flashes), and the removed node dims out (error). return: traverse from the dummy's next and show the remaining values.

lineExplanations.4: Start the next-pointer array (nextp[i] = the id node i points to).
lineExplanations.5: Loop over every real node to wire the chain.
lineExplanations.6: Every node except the last points to the following node.
lineExplanations.7: Point node i at node i+1.
lineExplanations.9: The last real node points at nothing (-1).
lineExplanations.10: Start the display-values array (parallels the nodes, plus the dummy).
lineExplanations.11: Copy each real node's value for display.
lineExplanations.14: The dummy node's id sits just past the real nodes.
lineExplanations.15: The dummy points at the head (node 0).
lineExplanations.16: changed marks whichever link just changed (for the animation).
lineExplanations.18: curr starts at the head to count nodes.
lineExplanations.21: Advance curr to the next node.
lineExplanations.22: Reset curr to the dummy for the second pass.
lineExplanations.23: The node to remove is (length - n) steps past the dummy.
lineExplanations.24: Track how many steps we've walked.
lineExplanations.25: Walk curr forward that many steps.
lineExplanations.28: removed is the node right after curr — the one to delete.
lineExplanations.29: Unlink it: curr's next skips over the removed node.
lineExplanations.31: The new head is whatever the dummy now points to.
lineExplanations.32: Start an empty list to read out the remaining values.
lineExplanations.33: Begin the read-out at the head.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def removeNthFromEnd(self, values, n): values are the node values; n counts from the end.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(sz): loops over each real node index.
syntaxExplanations.6: if i < sz - 1: true for every node except the last.
syntaxExplanations.7: nextp.append(i + 1) links node i to node i+1.
syntaxExplanations.8: else: handles the last node (bare else — no step of its own).
syntaxExplanations.9: nextp.append(-1) marks the tail's next as none.
syntaxExplanations.11: for i in range(sz): loops over each real node.
syntaxExplanations.12: disp_vals.append(values[i]) copies the value for display.
syntaxExplanations.13: disp_vals.append(0) adds the dummy sentinel's value.
syntaxExplanations.14: dummy = sz is the dummy node's id (just past the real nodes).
syntaxExplanations.15: nextp.append(0 if sz > 0 else -1) points the dummy at the head.
syntaxExplanations.16: changed = [] holds the id of the link that just changed.
syntaxExplanations.18: curr = 0 if sz > 0 else -1 starts the count at the head.
syntaxExplanations.19: while curr != -1: walk to the end.
syntaxExplanations.21: curr = nextp[curr] advances to the next node.
syntaxExplanations.22: curr = dummy resets to the dummy for pass two.
syntaxExplanations.23: steps = length - n is how far past the dummy the target sits.
syntaxExplanations.25: while moved < steps: walk exactly that many steps.
syntaxExplanations.26: curr = nextp[curr] advances curr one node.
syntaxExplanations.28: removed = nextp[curr] is the node to delete.
syntaxExplanations.31: head = nextp[dummy] is the (possibly new) head.
syntaxExplanations.32: order = [] will collect the remaining values.
syntaxExplanations.34: while cur2 != -1: traverse to the end.
syntaxExplanations.36: cur2 = nextp[cur2] follows the next pointer.
syntaxExplanations.37: return order hands back the remaining values.
